evals.prompt: Produce a QA strategy for a React SaaS product with 8 developers, no existing test infrastructure, continuous deployment, and monthly payment processing. We had one critical bug escape to production last quarter.
expected_patterns: PR gate AND merge gate AND deploy gate are all defined
phased rollout with Foundation, Coverage Expansion, and Quality Gates phases
anti_patterns: ice cream cone detection without action plan

evals.prompt: Assess a test suite that has 150 E2E tests, 50 integration tests, and 200 unit tests. CI takes 52 minutes. The team reports frequent flaky test failures and slow feedback loops.
expected_patterns: separate genuinely flaky tests from ones exposing real bugs before rebalancing
concrete target counts for the rebalanced pyramid

evals.prompt: Define entry and exit criteria for unit, integration, E2E, and release testing for a fintech payment platform.
expected_patterns: Exit:.*branches covered OR (edge cases tested) OR (coverage target met)
Entry: all test levels pass.*(CRITICAL|HIGH) defects
Exit: smoke tests pass.*monitoring shows no anomalies
anti_patterns: entry criteria without dependencies on earlier levels

evals.prompt: A startup has 4 developers, MVP launched 2 months ago, 5% defect escape rate to production, no QA team. Create a minimal but effective QA strategy.
expected_patterns: risk assessment focused on the 3-5 highest-impact features
realistic baseline metrics for a small team
anti_patterns: metric targets that require manual work beyond team capacity

evals.prompt: Document tool selection rationale for a Python API service. Candidates are pytest + testcontainers for integration, requests for API testing, k6 for load testing.
Create a risk assessment matrix for an e-commerce platform covering: payment processing, user authentication, product search, inventory management, and customer support chat.
expected_patterns: testing action mapped to each risk level
automation and monitoring recommendations per risk level
anti_patterns: no testing action per risk level

evals.prompt: Define quality gates for a SaaS product moving from monthly releases to weekly releases. What gates should pass before PR merge? Before production deploy?
expected_patterns: PR gate.*unit tests pass.*integration tests pass.*coverage does not decrease

evals.prompt: Set up a 16-week timeline and phased rollout for a testing strategy covering: CI pipeline setup, E2E test suite creation, coverage gates, and metrics dashboards.

You have a legacy monolith with 2000 unit tests and 50 E2E tests. Code coverage is 35%. Team is growing from 6 to 12 developers. Update the strategy to support team scale without extending CI time beyond 20 minutes.
expected_patterns: coverage targets scaled to the larger team capacity
integration testing gaps in the monolith architecture
integration test growth plan for the missing middle layer
anti_patterns: no mention of test dependency analysis
coverage targets unchanged for larger team

evals.prompt: Document three anti-patterns you detect in a strategy document that exists but is outdated. What are the triggers for quarterly review and re-evaluation?
expected_patterns: 100% coverage OR (ice cream cone) OR (one-time document)
tool-first OR (no metrics) OR (testing in isolation)
copy-paste OR (automating everything) OR (keep exploration manual)
